delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: This seminar builds and expands on the concepts we studied in Seminar 1. The course explores pragmatics within foreign language instruction. Students will read research in instructional pragmatics, analyze language use in context, and develop practical teaching applications. The course also emphasizes academic writing using process specific writing tasks.
aims: The aim of this Seminar is to build on the concepts learned in Seminar 1 and extend students’ understanding of instructional pragmatics in foreign language education. We will use critical discussion, project-based learning, and guided academic writing practice to help students learn to connect pragmatic theory with practical classroom applications.
goals: Students enrolled in this course will deepen their understanding of pragmatics and instructional pragmatics within the context of foreign language education.
Students enrolled in this course will examine key research and practical approaches for teaching pragmatic competence in EFL classrooms.
Students enrolled in this course will develop academic writing skills through a process writing approach, including drafting, peer feedback, revision, and reflection.

plan.text: Introduction - Course overview and review of key concepts from Seminar 1
Pragmatic development and challenges for EFL learners
Intercultural pragmatics and communication across contexts
Academic writing workshop: Introduction to academic writing
Pragmatic input and classroom materials analysis
Introduction to research in instructional pragmatics
Tutorial class: mid-semester review and discussion
Pragmatic assessment and feedback in language teaching
Academic writing workshop: summarizing and responding to research
Student project preparation and activity design (Part 1)
Student project presentations and peer feedback (Part 2)
Linking pragmatics theory to practical teaching contexts
Final project workshop: lesson plan and teaching rationale development
Final project presentations: instructional pragmatics applications

textbooks: All materials will be provided through Teams
prep: Students will be expected to complete all class readings and participate actively in English in each class.
teachers: ◎ デイナ リングリー
